title: ReDows ruleset file
description: One ruleset file: a schema version plus rules (with their layer), and/or app templates and their instantiations.

schema_version.description: Ruleset schema version understood by the engine. A file declaring a newer version is refused (fail-closed).

layer.description: Evaluation stage of every rule in this file (required when 'rules' is present). Stage order: carve_out → deny → capture; the first stage with a match decides.

rules.description: The classification rules of this file.

templates.description: Per-app templates (apps-ctt catalogue): a shared pattern (P-CHROM…) declared once, instantiated by 'apps' entries. Template rules carry their own layer.

apps.description: Template instantiations: each app expands its template into ordinary rules with ids 'app.<name>.<suffix>'.

id.description: Stable unique identifier, referenced by manifests and the review learning loop.

scope.description: Anchoring: machine (machine token), user (instantiated per profile), volume (volume root), drive (floating, must start with **/).

match.description: Bounded glob with '/' separators. First segment may be a location token: %SystemRoot%-style env token, FOLDERID_X Known Folder, or <UserProfile>. Absolute paths are forbidden.

verdict.description: Classification verdict. 'ignore with exceptions' is expressed with the exceptions list, not a separate verdict.

prio.description: Priority axis (orthogonal to the verdict). Default: normal.

bare_name_class.description: Required on floating bare-name ignore rules (scope drive + verdict ignore): collision_prone rules must carry a 'when' condition (deny-list §0-8).

when.description: Context condition gating the rule. When the condition is false the rule does not match.

exceptions.description: Nested exceptions with their own verdict, evaluated before this rule (deny-list §0-9).

source.description: Provenance: where this rule comes from (design note section, external source).

flags.description: Orthogonal flags (an axis, never encoded in the verdict). dpapi_machine_bound: the captured bytes are DPAPI-bound to this machine and unreadable after a reset — surfaced as a pre-reset alert (export/synchronize BEFORE).

id.description: Stable unique identifier (same namespace as rule ids).

match.description: Bounded glob, same grammar and anchoring as the parent rule. Must target paths inside the parent's zone.

flags.description: Orthogonal flags, same vocabulary as rule flags.

Condition.title: Condition
Condition.description: Exactly one of: all, any, ancestor_marker.

all.description: True when every child condition is true.

any.description: True when at least one child condition is true.

ancestor_marker.description: True when an ancestor directory of the item (up to the volume root) contains an entry matching one of these name globs (e.g. package.json, *.sln, .git).

params.description: Parameter names this template requires. Every '{param}' placeholder used in matches must be declared here.

rules.description: The rules this template expands to, one per app instantiation.

layer.description: Evaluation stage of this expanded rule (a template may mix stages: capture the profile, deny its caches).

match.description: Bounded glob; '{param}' placeholders are substituted with the app's param values at expansion.

flags.description: Orthogonal flags, same vocabulary as rule flags.

exceptions.description: Nested exceptions; their ids are suffixed like rule ids.

flags.description: Orthogonal flags, same vocabulary as rule flags.

App.title: App
App.description: One template instantiation: an application from the apps-ctt catalogue.

name.description: Application name, used in expanded rule ids ('app.<name>.<suffix>').

template.description: Name of the template to expand.

params.description: Values for the template's declared params (exactly the declared set).
